sections.title: Aromatic Regulation
content: Chikorita uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity of its surroundings. It loves to sunbathe.
It emits a sweet, refreshing aroma from its leaf that has the power to calm down battling Pokémon and create a friendly atmosphere.

sections.title: Botanical Design
content: Influenced by the long-necked Sauropod dinosaurs (specifically the Brachiosaurus), blended with a pear and an unfurling leaf.

sections.title: Combustion Vents
content: Cyndaquil is usually timid and curls into a ball when startled. It has several fire-emitting vents on its back.
If it is angry or surprised, it shoots flames out of its back. The intensity of these flames indicates its level of exhaustion.

sections.title: Mastication Reflex
content: Totodile has a playful but dangerous habit of biting anything it sees moving. Its jaws are powerful enough to crush heavy objects.
Even if its trainer is just trying to show affection, Totodile may accidentally cause injury with its powerful snap.

sections.title: Luminescent Guidance
content: The tip of Ampharos's tail glows so brightly that it can be seen from space. In ancient times, people used its light as a beacon for ships.
It was once heavily utilized in lighthouses along the Johto coast before the advent of industrial lighting.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A versatile Electric-type in the Ultra League. Its access to the fast-charging Volt Switch and the coverage move Brutal Swing or Focus Blast makes it a dangerous 'safe swap'.

sections.title: Psychic Sensitivity
content: Espeon is extremely loyal to any trainer it considers worthy. It is said to have developed its precognitive powers to protect its trainer from harm.
The fine fur covering its body is so sensitive that it can sense minute shifts in the air, allowing it to predict its opponent's next move and even the weather.

sections.title: Mythological Baseline
content: Inspired by the Bakeneko (monster cat) of Japanese folklore, specifically the Nekomata, which is often depicted with a forked tail.

sections.title: Nocturnal Synchronization
content: Umbreon evolved as a result of exposure to the moon's waves. It hides silently in darkness and waits for its foes to make a move.
The yellow rings on its body glow when it is excited or when it is exposed to moonlight, striking fear into the hearts of anyone nearby.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The definitive 'Tank' of the Great League. With astronomical defense and HP stats, Umbreon can outlast almost any opponent. Snarl provides elite energy generation for the community-day move Psychic or the heavy-hitting Foul Play.

sections.title: Metallic Exoskeleton
content: Scizor has a body with the hardness of steel. It is not easily fazed by ordinary attacks. It flaps its wings to regulate its body temperature.
Its pincers contain steel and can smash any hard object into bits. It raises its pincers and waves them around to scare off predators.

sections.title: Sap Obsession
content: Heracross is usually docile, but if it is disturbed while sipping honey or sap, it will use its powerful horn to hurl the intruder away.
It is capable of lifting objects more than 100 times its own weight with its impressive horn strength.

sections.title: Electromagnetic Velocity
content: Raikou embodies the speed of lightning. The roars it emits send shock waves shuddering through the air and shake the ground as if lightning bolts had struck.
It is said to have descended with lightning. It can fire electrical blasts from the rain clouds on its back.

sections.title: Insurmountable Durability
content: Tyranitar is so overwhelmingly powerful, it can bring down a whole mountain to make its nest. It wanders about in mountains seeking new opponents to fight.
Its body is practically indestructible, so it is extremely difficult to inflict any significant damage upon it.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A niche but terrifying pick in the Master League. Its 'Smack Down' fast move can melt Flying-types (like Ho-Oh or Lugia) in seconds, though its many weaknesses require careful team composition.

sections.title: Guardian of the Seas
content: Lugia's wings pack devastating power—a light fluttering of them can blow apart regular houses. As a result, it chooses to live out of sight deep under the sea.
It is said to be the guardian of the seas and is capable of calming the most violent storms.

sections.title: Celestial Resurrection
content: Ho-Oh's feathers glow in seven colors depending on the angle at which they are struck by light. These feathers are said to bring happiness to the bearers.
Legend says this Pokémon flew over the burned tower in Ecruteak City and resurrected three deceased Pokémon as the Legendary Beasts.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A top-tier Master League titan. Incinerate and Brave Bird allow it to output massive damage, while Sacred Fire (a signature move) provides a high chance to debuff the opponent's attack.
